title: acrolophus squarrosus
intro: Acrolophus squarrosus is a plant species, also known as the "prickly-head," which belongs to the family of flowering plants, Malpighiaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat various health issues. Its health benefits include being used as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory problems. Acrolophus squarrosus can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
